SLOC.definition: Source lines of code. The number of lines in a codebase that contain actual code, typically excluding comments and blank lines.

LoC.definition: Lines of Code. A (not very useful) software metric used to measure the size of a software program by counting the number of lines in its source code.

LLM.definition: Large Language Model. A type of artificial intelligence model that is trained on vast amounts of text data to understand and generate human-like language. These models are often used for code generation in software development.

century ride.definition: A long-distance cycling event covering 100 miles (approximately 160 kilometers).

useEffect.definition: A React Hook that allows you to perform side effects in function components.

Postgres.definition: An open-source relational database management system (RDBMS) that emphasizes extensibility and SQL compliance.

cURL.definition: A command-line tool and library for transferring data with URLs, supporting various protocols such as HTTP, HTTPS, FTP, and more.
